mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through expanded corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero signals immediate supply-side contraction that forces short-term equity repricing and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO MoM changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases during late-cycle expansions across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining FEDFUNDS and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate profit margins, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no significant positive predictive power for 60-day S&P 500 returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction below zero percent triggers immediate recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and force negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 registers negative growth over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-shock recovery phases signals rapid capacity utilization that lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
